hypothesis: If a two-stage transfer learning framework that pretrains EfficientNet-B0 on additive-free SEM images of NCM cathodes and then fine-tunes on additive-induced samples is used, then the model will achieve higher class-wise accuracy and F1 scores on the nine-class labels (three compositions × three aging states) than a model trained without stage-wise pretraining on the target data alone, because the pretraining transfers morphology-relevant features to improve generalization to the data-poor, additive-influenced domain.
